summary: The reproduction bundle is complete and targets upstream commit 7adbe8bffe4de18da0a9f90734455c9f4dec8f74, but ./reproduction/run.sh was not executed in this session because the local environment does not provide Docker. The exploit logic itself was validated separately with the same two-frame trigger against both the unpacked workspace copy and a fresh upstream clone.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should clone the pinned yamux source, build the helper harness, send the crafted two-frame WINDOW_UPDATE sequence to a server-side Connection, and observe the proof string WINDOW_UPDATE_OVERFLOW_PANIC_CONFIRMED after the server task panics at send-window overflow.
actual_observed_result: Did not execute ./reproduction/run.sh in this session because the Docker CLI/runtime is unavailable. Separate direct cargo-based validation against the same vulnerable code path and pinned upstream commit observed the expected proof string and the panic message "send window not to exceed u32".
verification_method: The bundled verify.sh script compiles a minimal harness against /target-repo/yamux, drives the two-frame attack sequence, and requires the exact proof string WINDOW_UPDATE_OVERFLOW_PANIC_CONFIRMED. The harness returns success only when the server task ends in a panic after processing the crafted WINDOW_UPDATE on a live stream.
limitations_or_blockers: Docker is not installed in the session environment, so ./reproduction/run.sh could not be executed end to end here.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out commit 7adbe8bffe4de18da0a9f90734455c9f4dec8f74, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
